zh.Kingston Lisle, Berkshire, England, Unit: 英國英格蘭波克夏金斯頓 李爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Hynton I於1437年出生在英國英格蘭波克夏金斯頓 李爾時，他的父親Sir John "The Younger" Hynton II19歲，母親Matilda Golaffre17歲。他和Margaret Dickson至少有1個兒子。他去世於1491年在英國波克夏享年54歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭波克夏。

nameMeanings.name: Hynton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name either from Hunton (North Yorkshire) or from Hunton (Hampshire). The Yorkshire placename derives from the Old English personal name Hūna + Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, while the Hampshire placename comes from Old English hund ‘hound’ (genitive plural hunda) + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. There is another place called Hunton in Kent, but it is unknown if it produced a surname.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Hynton I was born in 1437, in Kingston Lisle, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir John "The Younger" Hynton II, was 19 and his mother, Matilda Golaffre, was 17. He had at least 1 son with Margaret Dickson. He died in 1491, in Berkshire, England, at the age of 54, and was buried in Berkshire, England, United Kingdom.

Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Margaret Dickson. Il est décédé en 1491, à Berkshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 54 ans, et a été enterré à Berkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Hynton Iが1437年にイギリスイギリスバークシュアイアキングストン リスルで生まれた時，父Sir John "The Younger" Hynton IIは19歳，母Matilda Golaffreは17歳であった。彼はMargaret Dicksonとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1491年にイギリスバークシュアイアで54歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスバークシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Hynton I(이)가 1437년 영국 잉글랜드 버크실르 킨그스턴 리슬일에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John "The Younger" Hynton II(은)는 19세였고, 그의 어머니 Matilda Golaffre(은)는 17세였다. 그는 Margaret Dickson(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1491년 영국 버크실르에서 54세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 버크실르에 묻혔다.

У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Margaret Dickson. Он умер в 1491 г. в местности биркшир, Англия в возрасте 54 лет и был похоронен в местности биркшир, Англия, Великобритания.
